compiled_state.description:
# CHANGELOG.md: git-cliff, prepend-scoped generate-then-curate from conventional commits

## Why

No user-facing changelog exists: `docs/release-notes.md` is the agent-facing contract-change log, and GitHub Releases notes are auto-generated. Across nine surveyed Python tools, towncrier (pip/pytest) solves a many-contributor merge-conflict problem rebar doesn't have; ruff/uv generate from PR labels (needs a PR workflow rebar doesn't have); git-cliff converts the conventional-commit discipline rebar already pays for into a changelog. Keep-a-Changelog is the convergent shape (rich's CHANGELOG.md is the exemplar). Rejected alternatives — towncrier (double-entry ceremony), PR-label generation (no PRs), GitHub-Releases-only (no in-repo artifact) — recorded in cliff.toml header comments.

## What

- Add `cliff.toml`. Keys under their proper sections (a top-level key is silently ignored): `[git] conventional_commits = true`, `filter_unconventional = false`, plus a catch-all commit parser so mixed-history commits are kept. The normative `commit_parsers` mapping (each line individually grep-provable in the AC): `feat` → "Added"; `fix` → "Fixed"; breaking (`!`/BREAKING CHANGE) → "Breaking" (own group, listed first); `perf` → "Changed"; `refactor` → "Changed"; `docs` → "Documentation"; skipped: `build`, `ci`, `chore`, `test`.
- **Pin the tool with an explicit version variable:** Makefile gains `GIT_CLIFF_VERSION := <exact 2.x pin chosen at execution>` and a `make changelog` target that (a) errors clearly if `git-cliff` is missing or its `--version` mismatches the pin, and (b) errors if `VERSION` is not supplied. Install path documented in docs/releasing.md as `pipx install git-cliff==<pin>` (standalone Rust binary; NOT a pyproject dev extra).
- **Invocation interface (resolves the missing-parameter finding):** `make changelog VERSION=vX.Y.Z` → runs exactly `git cliff --unreleased --tag $(VERSION) --prepend CHANGELOG.md`. Full-file regeneration is forbidden after initial generation: the initial run (once, back through v0.1.0; ledger quality accepted for history) is executed manually and documented as one-time; thereafter only the prepend-scoped target is sanctioned, so committed hand-curated sections are never touched. The maintainer hand-curates the newly prepended section before tagging.
- Include `## [Unreleased]` and the compare-link block (rich pattern).
- `docs/releasing.md` gains the step: `make changelog VERSION=vX.Y.Z` → curate the top section (~5 min) → commit → tag; plus the pin + install line. Dial position recorded there: generate-then-edit (pure-generated rejected as ledger-like; mypy-style prose rejected as unsustainable solo).
- **Repoint `[project.urls]` Changelog** (required edit; currently `docs/release-notes.md`, pyproject.toml:78) → `https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md`.
- Pointer line at the top of `docs/release-notes.md`: "User-facing changelog: CHANGELOG.md; this file tracks agent-visible contract changes" (the epic's cross-link AC, owned here).
- Known consumer, acknowledged: `src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:310` classifies CHANGELOG-touching diffs as a hard-to-reverse surface, so curation commits score higher review impact — accepted; no code change.

## Scope

IN: `cliff.toml`, `CHANGELOG.md`, Makefile (`GIT_CLIFF_VERSION` + `changelog` target), `docs/releasing.md` step, pyproject urls repoint, release-notes pointer line.
OUT: towncrier/news fragments; changing commit conventions; back-editing published GitHub Releases; the auto-generated GitHub Releases notes (complementary, unchanged); any change to workflow_ops.py.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` exists — prove: `grep -c "^## \[" CHANGELOG.md` ≥ 8 and `grep -q "\[Unreleased\]" CHANGELOG.md` and a compare-link block at the bottom (`grep -q "compare/" CHANGELOG.md`).
- [ ] `cliff.toml` normative mapping — prove, each individually: `grep -q "feat" cliff.toml && grep -q "Added" cliff.toml`; `grep -q "fix" cliff.toml && grep -q "Fixed" cliff.toml`; `grep -qi "breaking" cliff.toml`; `grep -q "refactor" cliff.toml`; `grep -q "Documentation" cliff.toml`; skips present: `grep -q "chore" cliff.toml`; structure: `grep -A3 "\[git\]" cliff.toml | grep -q "filter_unconventional = false"`; catch-all parser present (a trailing `message = ".*"`-style rule) — prove: `grep -q '"\.\*"' cliff.toml`; header comments record the rejected alternatives — prove: `grep -qi "towncrier" cliff.toml`.
- [ ] Makefile interface — prove: `grep -q "GIT_CLIFF_VERSION" Makefile`; `grep -q -- "--unreleased" Makefile`; `grep -q -- "--prepend" Makefile`; `grep -q "VERSION" Makefile` with a guard that errors when unset (`grep -qi "VERSION is required\|VERSION not set" Makefile`); no bare full-regen invocation in the target.
- [ ] Idempotence of the sanctioned path: running `make changelog VERSION=<current>` twice with no new commits produces no second diff.
- [ ] docs/releasing.md — prove: `grep -q "git-cliff==" docs/releasing.md` (the literal pinned install spec) and `grep -q "make changelog" docs/releasing.md`.
- [ ] URL repoint + pointer — prove: `grep "^Changelog" pyproject.toml | grep -q "CHANGELOG.md"`; `head -5 docs/release-notes.md | grep -q "CHANGELOG.md"`.
compiled_state.title: CHANGELOG.md: git-cliff generate-then-curate from conventional commits
